claim: Focus on capabilities over tools - mastering specific frameworks becomes obsolete quickly; instead focus on understanding what AI can do across tools
actionable: Prioritize learning core concepts (context engineering, prompting, agent architecture) over spending weeks mastering specific tools like Crew AI or Pantic.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: AI agents will be the dominant paradigm in 2025, replacing direct tool use with multi-agent orchestration
actionable: Shift focus from single-agent tools to understanding agent orchestration patterns and how to compose multiple specialized agents.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: Reasoning LLMs (like O3, O1, Qwen QwQ) are solving hallucination and poor decision-making problems that plagued earlier models
actionable: Evaluate reasoning LLMs for complex tasks that require chain-of-thought; combine them with smaller fast LLMs for latency-sensitive operations.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: Gap between closed-source LLMs (Claude, GPT) and open-source LLMs (Qwen, Llama) is shrinking rapidly, especially with models like DeepSeek V3
actionable: Evaluate open-source models for your use case; they offer fine-tuning, privacy, and cost advantages that may outweigh slightly lower quality.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: Local LLM advantages include: fine-tuning on custom data, data privacy, cost savings, speed, and flexibility with no API rate limits
actionable: For privacy-critical or high-volume use cases, evaluate locally-hosted LLMs to avoid API costs and data transmission.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: Building with AI requires understanding a tech stack across LLMs, hosting, databases, retrieval, coding assistance, and agent frameworks
actionable: Map out your AI tech stack: choose an LLM provider (cloud vs. local), hosting solution, vector DB, coding assistant, and orchestration framework.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: Keep AI tech stacks simple (KISS) and reusable (DRY) - avoid overthinking tool selection and find technologies that work across multiple use cases
actionable: Choose 3-5 core tools that can be reused for different projects; don't build complex stacks that require maintaining many integrations.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: Massive context windows (millions of tokens) enable new capabilities like putting entire documents into prompts as immediate context
actionable: Design prompts that leverage multi-megabyte context windows; include relevant reference documents, code, or conversation history directly in the prompt.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: Human-in-the-loop is critical for agents - agents need human approval for certain actions and this is a solvable design pattern
actionable: Use agent frameworks (Lang Graph) that support blocking operations where humans approve before agents proceed.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025

claim: Prompt engineering is a foundational skill including single-shot, multi-shot, and chain-of-thought techniques that apply across all LLMs
actionable: Master core prompting techniques that transfer across models rather than memorizing prompts for specific tools.
source_title: How to DOMINATE with AI in 2025
